steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail icon in the dock. The Settings app is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Settings app → | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings | Step 3: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete

[Decision]
To turn on Wi-Fi, I need to access the system settings. Since the Settings app icon is not present on the current home screen view, I will use a system-level command to open the Settings app directly to proceed with the network configuration.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen is the Settings page. Visible options include "Network & internet" (with subtext "Mobile, Wi-Fi, hotspot"), "Connected devices", "Apps", "Notifications", "Battery", "Storage", and "Sound & vibration".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Settings app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet settings → | Step 3: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the current plan step to navigate to network settings, I see the "Network & internet" option at the top of the list, which explicitly mentions Wi-Fi in its description. I will tap on this option to proceed to the Wi-Fi configuration page.

[Memory]
Target: Turn on Wi-Fi. Currently in Settings app, proceeding to Network & internet section.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Network & internet" settings page. Visible options include "Internet" (Networks available), "Calls & SMS", "SIMs", "Airplane mode" (currently off), "Hotspot & tethering", and "Data Saver". There is no direct Wi-Fi toggle on this screen; instead, Wi-Fi settings are grouped under the "Internet" option.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed Step 3 would be a direct "Turn on Wi-Fi toggle". However, in this Android version, Wi-Fi is managed under the "Internet" menu rather than having a standalone toggle on the Network & internet page.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open Settings ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet ✓ | Step 3: Tap on 'Internet' to access Wi-Fi settings → | Step 4: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete

[Decision]
Based on the updated plan, I need to access the Wi-Fi settings. Since there is no direct Wi-Fi toggle here, I must tap on "Internet" which lists "Networks available" underneath it, as this is where Wi-Fi networks and the Wi-Fi switch are located. I will tap on "Internet".

[Memory]
Target: Turn on Wi-Fi. In Android 10+ versions, Wi-Fi settings are nested under the "Internet" menu in Network & internet settings. Proceeding to find the toggle.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Internet" settings page. The key visible element is the "Wi-Fi" section, which has a toggle switch next to it. The toggle is currently in the off position (greyed out). Below it are options for "Network preferences", "Saved networks" (1 network), and "Non-carrier data usage".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Settings ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet ✓ | Step 3: Tap on 'Internet' to access Wi-Fi settings ✓ | Step 4: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete →

[Decision]
Based on the current plan step to turn on Wi-Fi, I can see the Wi-Fi toggle switch on the screen. It is currently off. I need to tap this toggle switch to turn it on and complete the user's request.

[Memory]
Target: Turn on Wi-Fi. Reached the Internet settings page where the Wi-Fi toggle is located. Wi-Fi toggle state was previously off. Once toggled on, the task should be complete.

[Observation]
The current screen is the "Internet" settings page. The Wi-Fi toggle switch is now in the "on" position (blue and switched to the right). Below it, a saved network "AndroidWifi" is visible, indicating Wi-Fi is active and ready to connect.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Settings ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Network & internet ✓ | Step 3: Tap on 'Internet' to access Wi-Fi settings ✓ | Step 4: Turn on Wi-Fi toggle, task complete ✓

[Decision]
Based on the observation, the Wi-Fi toggle is now in the active (on) state, which fulfills the user's instruction to "Turn wifi on". There are no further actions required. I will declare the task complete.

[Memory]
Target: Turn on Wi-Fi. Wi-Fi toggle state has been successfully changed to on. Task accomplished.
